creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The system generates reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap without requiring engineering requests.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that allows websites to expose structured, named, typed tools to AI agents instead of requiring them to parse the DOM and perform pixel-level interactions. It offers both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for defining agent-callable actions, and is currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser describes a workflow system that dispatches agents via voice briefs to isolated git worktrees while the user is offline, using signal agents to monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates for code quality and behavior, weekly analysis of conversation history for skill refinement, and biometric feedback integration via MCP. The design prioritizes asynchronous execution and human attention preservation rather than raw tool speed.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford demonstrates that a 4B model trained via RL with Snorkel achieved better SQL reasoning than Qwen 3 235B by learning tool discipline, including schema inspection and self-correction. Single-table training transferred to multi-table tasks, improving FinQA from 13.9% to 26.6%, with training cost under $500.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
videos.summary: Cole Medin conducts a live stream evaluating Anthropic's Fable 5 model against Claude Opus 4.8. The stream tests whether Fable 5 justifies its 2x price premium and the hype surrounding it as Anthropic's most capable shipped model.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that enables developers to build, test, and deploy AI agents to production quickly. The tool provides identity management, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade agent deployment and works with any AI coding assistant.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream doubles as casual conversation alongside chess gameplay.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that pairs Claude Opus for reasoning and planning with Gemini 3.5 Flash for frontend design and UI generation. He argues this composition approach outperforms picking a single model, leveraging Gemini's superior visual output quality at lower cost while retaining Opus for complex integration and judgment tasks.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to architect proactive AI agents that operate in self-improving loops. It covers the design of autonomous feedback systems, demonstrates use cases in ads optimization and SEO, and references Loopany as an enabling framework with memory and tools.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses autonomous coding tools and workflows, focusing on the /goal feature for code generation tasks and /Mission exploration for long-horizon tasks. The video promotes Crewlet early access and Superdesign as practical tools for autonomous coding workflows.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers how Symphony works, harness setup with auto-testing capabilities, and workflow configuration for development.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews several AI agent frameworks and techniques including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, Claude Code's 3-layer memory system, Hermes' self-evolution mechanism, and Openclaw's self-evolving skills. The video demonstrates agent capabilities through practical examples and comparisons.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series including Nano, Super, and Ultra model variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, modular test-time compute, and Latent Mixture of Experts architectures to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a large language model that adopts sparse attention (MSA) instead of full attention for improved inference efficiency. The model optimizes KV cache reads, tiling, and I/O patterns to reduce computational cost during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Pi, described as a unique agent framework that enables diverse use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi's approach to other frontier agents including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, examining how the harness layer becomes more complex as products add agent features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the semiconductor race between China and the USA. The advancement relies on improvements in EUV and DUV lithography, packaging techniques, and logic folding approaches to achieve smaller process nodes.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in 2026 as AI transforms hiring and technical roles. The content advocates that networking, personal branding, and continuous learning remain valuable, and argues that junior developers still have meaningful roles despite AI advancement.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production best practices for deploying AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The patterns apply across platforms but are demonstrated using AWS services and the Strands Agents SDK.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial from AWS Developers showing how to build a serverless REST API on AWS using the Cloud Development Kit (CDK) with Python. The video contrasts manual AWS console configuration (ClickOps) with Infrastructure as Code approach, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway deployment through CDK.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five techniques for reducing AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to reduce context window size, graph RAG for precise aggregations and multi-hop reasoning, multi-agent validation for error catching, symbolic guardrails implemented as code hooks, and runtime guardrails for agent self-correction. The techniques reduce token waste, improve accuracy, and catch failures before users encounter them.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video examining whether Google engineers use informal or exploratory code generation practices. The video compares code generation approaches between OpenAI Codex and Claude, and discusses coding patterns at Google across multiple years (2024-2026).

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech exploring how the Malus platform is changing open source maintainability and sustainability. The content discusses AI agents, open source practices, and the economic pressures facing maintainers in the modern software ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech ran an experiment comparing Claude Code's job performance against a human developer, documenting where the AI succeeded and failed. The video explores the workflow mechanics and results, raising questions about AI-driven job displacement.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that rabid fans and formal interviews are poor indicators, and instead advocates for embedded customer relationships and observing pull-based signals like pricing inquiries. He emphasizes betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird founded Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform for AI tools, raising $26M by betting on advertising rather than subscription models. He argues that the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust adoption, not technology, and that ads can provide better user experiences than subscriptions for AI applications.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid shares his bootstrapped playbook for growing Chatbase to $10M ARR without external funding. The video covers bootstrapping philosophy, product-led growth, customer retention, SEO strategy, pricing experimentation, and decision-making frameworks for early-stage SaaS founders.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh argues that teaching AI through manual, mathematical foundations at human speed provides more durable skills than chasing latest tools. He contends that focusing on fundamentals rather than trendy frameworks helps practitioners survive hype cycles and that AI adoption requires human adaptation rather than the reverse.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Agent Loop is Here. And it Changes Everything - Here's How.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof presents a video overview on AI agent loops, covering Fable 5 versus Mythos 5 models, loop engineering and recursive self-improvement techniques, memory systems for long-running tasks, and emerging opportunities in agentic payments and AI builders.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
